Native Browser API

Web Barcode Reader

Scan barcodes with your camera using the native BarcodeDetector API. No plugins, no uploads — runs entirely in your browser.

Tap Start to scan
BarcodeDetector API not supported in this browser. Try Chrome or Edge.

Try scanning these

Point your camera at any of these — they work with the scanner above.

QR Code

https://web-viivakoodi.vercel.app/

EAN-13

5901234123457

Code 128

WEB-BARCODE-128

Code 39

CODE39

EAN-8

96385074

Minimal Setup

Everything you need to read barcodes with a camera — under 25 lines.

HTML 
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8" />
  <title>Barcode Scanner</title>
</head>
<body>
  <video id="v" autoplay muted playsinline style="width:100%"></video>

  <script>
    const video = document.getElementById('v');

    navigator.mediaDevices
      .getUserMedia({ video: { facingMode: 'environment' } })
      .then(stream => (video.srcObject = stream));

    const detector = new BarcodeDetector({
      formats: ['qr_code', 'ean_13', 'code_128']
    });

    function scan() {
      if (video.paused) return;
      detector.detect(video).then(codes => {
        if (codes.length > 0) {
          alert(codes[0].format + ': ' + codes[0].rawValue);
          video.pause();
          setTimeout(() => video.play(), 2000);
        }
      });
      requestAnimationFrame(scan);
    }

    video.addEventListener('play', () => setTimeout(scan, 500));
  </script>
</body>
</html>

About the API

⚡

Native Performance

Runs in the browser using hardware-accelerated image processing — no JavaScript barcode libraries needed.

🔒

Privacy First

Camera frames never leave your device. All detection happens locally with zero network requests.

📦

Zero Dependencies

One native API call. No libraries, no WebAssembly bundles, no polyfills. Works in Chrome and Edge.

🔍

11 Formats

QR Code, EAN-13, EAN-8, Code 128, Code 39, Code 93, Codabar, ITF, PDF417, UPC-E, Data Matrix.

Open source · MIT License · BarcodeDetector MDN docs
